title: Steakhouse Steaks
description: From Barefoot Contessa How Easy Is That? — Ina's foolproof method for restaurant-quality steaks at home. The secret is a screaming-hot cast iron pan, a quick sear, and finishing in the oven. Topped with a compound herb butter that melts into the meat, this is the centerpiece of her Father's Day dinner menu.

ingredients.item: Bone-in rib-eye steaks, 1½ inches thick (about 1 lb each)
Unsalted butter, at room temperature (for compound butter)
Freshly ground black pepper (for compound butter)

instructions: Make the compound butter: Beat the butter with an electric mixer until fluffy. Add the garlic, parsley, chives, thyme, salt, and pepper and mix until combined. Shape into a log on plastic wrap, roll up, and refrigerate until firm, at least 30 minutes.
Preheat the oven to 400°F. Heat a large cast iron skillet over high heat for 5 to 7 minutes — it should be very, very hot.
Pat the steaks dry with paper towels. Brush both sides with olive oil and season very generously with salt and pepper (use more than you think you need).

Place the steaks in the hot skillet and sear for exactly 2 minutes on each side without moving them. Transfer the skillet to the oven and cook for 6 to 8 minutes for medium-rare (125°F internal temperature).
Remove from the oven, place a thick slice of compound butter on each steak, and tent loosely with foil. Rest for 10 minutes before serving.
tips: The cast iron pan MUST be screaming hot before the steaks go in — this is what creates the crust.
Pat the steaks completely dry — moisture is the enemy of a good sear.
Season more aggressively than you think necessary — thick steaks need a lot of salt.
The compound butter can be made weeks ahead and frozen — slice off rounds as needed.
Use an instant-read thermometer: 125°F for medium-rare, 135°F for medium.
Source: Barefoot Contessa How Easy Is That? by Ina Garten (2010)
